qa.user_verbatim_complaint: 修复 hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh 复合命令绕过 — Single ls/stat/cat reading protected path 仍然 ALLOW; compound `ls <flag> ; <write>` / `&&` / `||` / `|` / `$(...)` / backtick + write 必须 DENY (exit 2). 扩展或新建 test_AC_04_d4e6f8a0c3b5d7ea.py 覆盖复合命令旁路。
qa.overall_assessment: BA iter-3 successfully closes the 2 NEW iter-2 spec_text_vs_execution_drift blockers (B8 C18 + B9 C26) and addresses O4 matrix-prose-count nit via 3 surgical mechanical edits — no scope change, no new ACs, no regression to iter-2 evidence. (1) C18 payload swapped from `find <flag> -exec python3 -c '...' {} +` to `find <flag> -exec touch {} +` — `touch` is not in M1 bullet 4 (language -c/-e) denylist, so the case now ISOLATES M1 bullet 6 (find -exec rule); only M1 bullet 6 can drive the rc=0→rc=2 transition. (2) C26 payload swapped from `python3 -c '...write...' # scripts/...` to `touch <flag> # scripts/...` — same isolation logic, so M2 substring-tightening (STARTS-WITH vs CONTAINS) is now the ONLY rule that can drive C26's rc transition. (3) O4 fixed: 5 active-prose references say `33-row matrix`; the single remaining `16-case matrix` occurrence is in the iter-3 revision-history note at ticket line 8 describing the swap (legitimate historical reference, not active prose). All 5 mechanical checks pass empirically against the live artifacts; verdict flips iter-2 fail → iter-3 pass.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.notes: Iter-2 drifts B8 (C18) and B9 (C26) both resolved. C18 isolating payload `find /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-testsid.flag -exec touch {} +` contains NO `-c`/`-e` token, so M1 bullet 4 (language -c/-e) cannot independently drive the rc transition; only M1 bullet 6 (find -exec rule) can. C26 isolating payload `touch /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-testsid.flag # scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py` contains NO `python3 -c`, so M1 bullet 4 cannot intercept; only M2 substring-tightening (STARTS-WITH vs CONTAINS branch (a) match) can drive the transition. Both cases now exercise the rule each was labeled to test. Per-case rationale fields and _iter3_payload_swapped_for fields correctly cite the QA B8/B9 lineage.

non_blocking_observations.issue: Ticket line 288 (Technical Hints section) said 'AC-01's 16-case matrix' even though the binding AC requires 33 rows.
non_blocking_observations.status_iter3: resolved — iter-3 ticket has `33-row matrix` at line 289 (and 4 other active-prose locations: 127, 170, 241, 243). The single remaining `16-case matrix` at line 8 is in the iter-3 revision-history note describing the swap (legitimate historical reference).

non_blocking_observations.issue: C17 (`ls <flag> | xargs touch`) labeled as xargs exhibitor also contains `|` which is independently denied by M1 bullet 2. However: in this case, M1 bullet 6 explicitly enumerates xargs alongside the separators-list as a 'no nested write surface' rule, and BOTH bullet 2 and bullet 6 are legitimate M1 clauses — the rule semantics overlap by design. Unlike C18/C26, this overlap is not a drift because xargs in shell almost always requires a separator/redirect anyway; an isolating xargs case is genuinely difficult to construct. QA notes for transparency; not a blocker.

C22-C25 (newline, bare `&`, `|&`, if/then) all use `python3 -c` as the write side. Like C18/C26, this means a Dev fix that implements only M1 bullet 4 could pass them without implementing the specific separator rule. However: each of C22-C25's LABELED rule is one of the no-control-structure clauses in M1 bullet 2, and BA's M1 implementation pattern (single anchored regex over all separators) makes it unlikely Dev would land bullet 2 partially. QA observes for transparency; not blocking because (a) M1 bullet 2 is one regex block in the recommended implementation, so partial landing is implausible; (b) the same write side `python3 -c` is reused across all four cases, which makes drift across all four simultaneously a single risk, not four independent risks. If BA wants belt-and-suspenders coverage, isolating variants using `touch` as the write side would close this observation. Non-blocking because C18 and C26 are independently sufficient to FAIL the iter-2 validation.
